title: If you love me you will keep my commandments
author: Alexis Sosa Ramos
excerpt: What is KEEP in Jesus claim regarding the Commandments?
content:
Jesus said if you love me you will keep my commandments - John 14:15

The argument is that people often refer to this as the only way of showing love to Jesus, and if we dont do it we dont love him.

Or in other debates is that Christians dont do the commandments or laws that are given to Moses.

The argument against it is the following,

To keep and to fufill are two different things, if we go back to the word tēreō which is "keep" the definition clearly gives the understanding of what Jesus clearly meant which is: to guard, keep watch, preserve, or observe.

In short, we cant fufill the law because of our sinful nature, but we are commanded to (if we love Jesus) to guard it, to keep watch of it, to preserve it, to observe it.

So if anyone tells you that you dont do the law, remind them these passages:
Mark 10:18
Romans 3:23
Matthew 5:21-22
